% Options for packages loaded elsewhere
\PassOptionsToPackage{unicode}{hyperref}
\PassOptionsToPackage{hyphens}{url}
\documentclass[
  ]{article}
\usepackage{xcolor}
\usepackage{amsmath,amssymb}
% start of pandoc_writing_tools-specific template part
\newtheorem{definition}{Definition}
\newtheorem{example}{Example}
\usepackage{makeidx}
\makeindex
\newcounter{TodoCounter}
\usepackage[backgroundcolor=white,linecolor=black]{todonotes}
\let\oldtodo\todo
\usepackage{bclogo}%  \bcpanchant
\newcommand{\todospan}[1]{
  \stepcounter{TodoCounter}
  \oldtodo[caption={\arabic{TodoCounter}. #1}]{\bcpanchant #1}
}
\newcommand{\tododiv}[1]{
  \stepcounter{TodoCounter}
  \oldtodo[inline,caption={\arabic{TodoCounter}. #1}]{\bcpanchant \textit{#1}}
}
\usepackage{caption}
\captionsetup[figure]{labelformat=empty}
\usepackage{changepage} % for adjustwidth environment
% end of pandoc_writing_tools-specific template part
\setcounter{secnumdepth}{5}
\usepackage{iftex}
\ifPDFTeX
  \usepackage[T1]{fontenc}
  \usepackage[utf8]{inputenc}
  \usepackage{textcomp} % provide euro and other symbols
\else % if luatex or xetex
  \usepackage{unicode-math} % this also loads fontspec
  \defaultfontfeatures{Scale=MatchLowercase}
  \defaultfontfeatures[\rmfamily]{Ligatures=TeX,Scale=1}
\fi
\usepackage{lmodern}
\ifPDFTeX\else
  % xetex/luatex font selection
\fi
% Use upquote if available, for straight quotes in verbatim environments
\IfFileExists{upquote.sty}{\usepackage{upquote}}{}
\IfFileExists{microtype.sty}{% use microtype if available
  \usepackage[]{microtype}
  \UseMicrotypeSet[protrusion]{basicmath} % disable protrusion for tt fonts
}{}
\makeatletter
\@ifundefined{KOMAClassName}{% if non-KOMA class
  \IfFileExists{parskip.sty}{%
    \usepackage{parskip}
  }{% else
    \setlength{\parindent}{0pt}
    \setlength{\parskip}{6pt plus 2pt minus 1pt}}
}{% if KOMA class
  \KOMAoptions{parskip=half}}
\makeatother
\usepackage{graphicx}
\makeatletter
\newsavebox\pandoc@box
\newcommand*\pandocbounded[1]{% scales image to fit in text height/width
  \sbox\pandoc@box{#1}%
  \Gscale@div\@tempa{\textheight}{\dimexpr\ht\pandoc@box+\dp\pandoc@box\relax}%
  \Gscale@div\@tempb{\linewidth}{\wd\pandoc@box}%
  \ifdim\@tempb\p@<\@tempa\p@\let\@tempa\@tempb\fi% select the smaller of both
  \ifdim\@tempa\p@<\p@\scalebox{\@tempa}{\usebox\pandoc@box}%
  \else\usebox{\pandoc@box}%
  \fi%
}
% Set default figure placement to htbp
\def\fps@figure{htbp}
\makeatother
% definitions for citeproc citations
\NewDocumentCommand\citeproctext{}{}
\NewDocumentCommand\citeproc{mm}{%
  \begingroup\def\citeproctext{#2}\cite{#1}\endgroup}
\makeatletter
 % allow citations to break across lines
 \let\@cite@ofmt\@firstofone
 % avoid brackets around text for \cite:
 \def\@biblabel#1{}
 \def\@cite#1#2{{#1\if@tempswa , #2\fi}}
\makeatother
\newlength{\cslhangindent}
\setlength{\cslhangindent}{1.5em}
\newlength{\csllabelwidth}
\setlength{\csllabelwidth}{3em}
\newenvironment{CSLReferences}[2] % #1 hanging-indent, #2 entry-spacing
 {\begin{list}{}{%
  \setlength{\itemindent}{0pt}
  \setlength{\leftmargin}{0pt}
  \setlength{\parsep}{0pt}
  % turn on hanging indent if param 1 is 1
  \ifodd #1
   \setlength{\leftmargin}{\cslhangindent}
   \setlength{\itemindent}{-1\cslhangindent}
  \fi
  % set entry spacing
  \setlength{\itemsep}{#2\baselineskip}}}
 {\end{list}}
\usepackage{calc}
\newcommand{\CSLBlock}[1]{\hfill\break\parbox[t]{\linewidth}{\strut\ignorespaces#1\strut}}
\newcommand{\CSLLeftMargin}[1]{\parbox[t]{\csllabelwidth}{\strut#1\strut}}
\newcommand{\CSLRightInline}[1]{\parbox[t]{\linewidth - \csllabelwidth}{\strut#1\strut}}
\newcommand{\CSLIndent}[1]{\hspace{\cslhangindent}#1}
\setlength{\emergencystretch}{3em} % prevent overfull lines
\providecommand{\tightlist}{%
  \setlength{\itemsep}{0pt}\setlength{\parskip}{0pt}}
\usepackage{bookmark}
\IfFileExists{xurl.sty}{\usepackage{xurl}}{} % add URL line breaks if available
\urlstyle{same}
\hypersetup{
  pdftitle={pandoc\_writing\_tools Feature Demo},
  hidelinks,
  pdfcreator={LaTeX via pandoc}}

\title{pandoc\_writing\_tools Feature Demo}
\author{}
\date{}

\begin{document}
\maketitle

% start of pandoc_writing_tools-specific template part
%\clearpage
%
%\vspace*{\fill}
%\includegraphics[height=2cm]{example-image}


Last updated: <DATE>

Version <VER>
%\clearpage
% end of pandoc_writing_tools-specific template part

{
\setcounter{tocdepth}{3}
\tableofcontents
}
\section{\texorpdfstring{Overview{
\href{mailto:docs@example.com?subject=\%5BFeedback\%5D\%20pandoc\_writing\_tools\%20Feature\%20Demo\%20v<VER>\%20-\%20Section\%3A\%20Overview\&body=Hi\%2C\%0D\%0A\%0D\%0AI\%20have\%20some\%20feedback\%20regarding\%20the\%20section\%20\%27Overview\%27\%20in\%20\%27pandoc\_writing\_tools\%20Feature\%20Demo\%27\%20v<VER>.\%0D\%0A\%0D\%0A}{\scriptsize\texttt{[Give Feedback]}}}}{Overview }}\label{sec:overview}

This document demonstrates the functionality of the Lua filters in
pandoc\_writing\_tools.

\subsection{\texorpdfstring{Citations and section references{
\href{mailto:docs@example.com?subject=\%5BFeedback\%5D\%20pandoc\_writing\_tools\%20Feature\%20Demo\%20v<VER>\%20-\%20Section\%3A\%20Citations\%20and\%20section\%20references\&body=Hi\%2C\%0D\%0A\%0D\%0AI\%20have\%20some\%20feedback\%20regarding\%20the\%20section\%20\%27Citations\%20and\%20section\%20references\%27\%20in\%20\%27pandoc\_writing\_tools\%20Feature\%20Demo\%27\%20v<VER>.\%0D\%0A\%0D\%0A}{\scriptsize\texttt{[Give Feedback]}}}}{Citations and section references }}\label{sec:citations-and-refs}

This is what a citation looks like: (Doe 2020). The list of all cited
references appears in the bibliography section at the end of the
document.

The pandoc\_writing\_tools lua filters enable referring to a section by
number, for example, this references section \hyperref[sec:overview]{1}.

\subsection{\texorpdfstring{Table of contents{
\href{mailto:docs@example.com?subject=\%5BFeedback\%5D\%20pandoc\_writing\_tools\%20Feature\%20Demo\%20v<VER>\%20-\%20Section\%3A\%20Table\%20of\%20contents\&body=Hi\%2C\%0D\%0A\%0D\%0AI\%20have\%20some\%20feedback\%20regarding\%20the\%20section\%20\%27Table\%20of\%20contents\%27\%20in\%20\%27pandoc\_writing\_tools\%20Feature\%20Demo\%27\%20v<VER>.\%0D\%0A\%0D\%0A}{\scriptsize\texttt{[Give Feedback]}}}}{Table of contents }}\label{table-of-contents}

The table of contents is generated from the section headers. The
\texttt{toc-list-top-level} and \texttt{toc-list-entry-levels}
attributes control which headers are included and how they are nested.
For example, the mini-TOC below includes level 2 and level 3 headers
under the ``Overview'' section:

\hyperref[sec:citations-and-refs]{1.1 Citations and section references }

\hyperref[table-of-contents]{1.2 Table of contents }

\hyperref[sec:figures]{1.3 Figures and references }

\hyperref[sec:examples]{1.4 Examples and definitions }

\hyperref[sec:index-entries]{1.5 Index entries }

\hyperref[sec:sidenotes]{1.6 Sidenotes }

\hyperref[sec:todos]{1.7 TODOs }

\hyperref[sec:github-links]{1.8 Linking to github issues and edit links
}

\subsection{\texorpdfstring{Figures and references{
\href{mailto:docs@example.com?subject=\%5BFeedback\%5D\%20pandoc\_writing\_tools\%20Feature\%20Demo\%20v<VER>\%20-\%20Section\%3A\%20Figures\%20and\%20references\&body=Hi\%2C\%0D\%0A\%0D\%0AI\%20have\%20some\%20feedback\%20regarding\%20the\%20section\%20\%27Figures\%20and\%20references\%27\%20in\%20\%27pandoc\_writing\_tools\%20Feature\%20Demo\%27\%20v<VER>.\%0D\%0A\%0D\%0A}{\scriptsize\texttt{[Give Feedback]}}}}{Figures and references }}\label{sec:figures}

\begin{figure}
\centering
\pandocbounded{\includegraphics[keepaspectratio]{img/demo-figure.pdf}}
\caption{Figure 1: A demo figure}\label{fig:demo}
\end{figure}

See Figure \hyperref[fig:demo]{1} for the numbered figure reference.

\subsection{\texorpdfstring{Examples and definitions{
\href{mailto:docs@example.com?subject=\%5BFeedback\%5D\%20pandoc\_writing\_tools\%20Feature\%20Demo\%20v<VER>\%20-\%20Section\%3A\%20Examples\%20and\%20definitions\&body=Hi\%2C\%0D\%0A\%0D\%0AI\%20have\%20some\%20feedback\%20regarding\%20the\%20section\%20\%27Examples\%20and\%20definitions\%27\%20in\%20\%27pandoc\_writing\_tools\%20Feature\%20Demo\%27\%20v<VER>.\%0D\%0A\%0D\%0A}{\scriptsize\texttt{[Give Feedback]}}}}{Examples and definitions }}\label{sec:examples}

We have custom environments for examples and definitions, which are
numbered and can be referenced from the text:

\protect\phantomsection\label{ex:demo}
\begin{example}

\label{ex:demo}

\textup{Demo example.}

This block is tagged as an example.

\end{example}

\protect\phantomsection\label{def:demo}
\begin{definition}

\label{def:demo}

\textup{Demo definition.}

This block is tagged as a definition.

\end{definition}

Refer back to Example \hyperref[ex:demo]{1} and Definition
\hyperref[def:demo]{1}.

\subsection{\texorpdfstring{Index entries{
\href{mailto:docs@example.com?subject=\%5BFeedback\%5D\%20pandoc\_writing\_tools\%20Feature\%20Demo\%20v<VER>\%20-\%20Section\%3A\%20Index\%20entries\&body=Hi\%2C\%0D\%0A\%0D\%0AI\%20have\%20some\%20feedback\%20regarding\%20the\%20section\%20\%27Index\%20entries\%27\%20in\%20\%27pandoc\_writing\_tools\%20Feature\%20Demo\%27\%20v<VER>.\%0D\%0A\%0D\%0A}{\scriptsize\texttt{[Give Feedback]}}}}{Index entries }}\label{sec:index-entries}

The index filter allows you to mark index entries in the text, which are
then collected and emitted in an index div, see at the end of this
document. The simplest usage is to just add \texttt{.index} spans around
the relevant text and add an index div somewhere in the document. The
filter will collect all \texttt{.index} spans and emit them as entries
in the index div. You can also specify an \texttt{entry} attribute on
the span to customize the index entry text, and use \texttt{!} to create
nested entries.

For example, in this sentence the word
\protect\phantomsection\label{__index_entry_1}{color}\index{color} is
indexed, and a we index the word
``\protect\phantomsection\label{__index_entry_2}{concept}\index{idea}''
as ``idea''. We can also create nested entries like
\protect\phantomsection\label{__index_entry_3}{color!blue}\index{color!blue}\index{color!blue},
and multiple entries like
\protect\phantomsection\label{__index_entry_4}{alpha;beta}\index{alpha}\index{beta}.

\subsection{\texorpdfstring{Sidenotes{
\href{mailto:docs@example.com?subject=\%5BFeedback\%5D\%20pandoc\_writing\_tools\%20Feature\%20Demo\%20v<VER>\%20-\%20Section\%3A\%20Sidenotes\&body=Hi\%2C\%0D\%0A\%0D\%0AI\%20have\%20some\%20feedback\%20regarding\%20the\%20section\%20\%27Sidenotes\%27\%20in\%20\%27pandoc\_writing\_tools\%20Feature\%20Demo\%27\%20v<VER>.\%0D\%0A\%0D\%0A}{\scriptsize\texttt{[Give Feedback]}}}}{Sidenotes }}\label{sec:sidenotes}

This sentence has a footnote.\footnote{Footnote content that becomes a
  sidenote in HTML.}

\subsection{\texorpdfstring{TODOs{
\href{mailto:docs@example.com?subject=\%5BFeedback\%5D\%20pandoc\_writing\_tools\%20Feature\%20Demo\%20v<VER>\%20-\%20Section\%3A\%20TODOs\&body=Hi\%2C\%0D\%0A\%0D\%0AI\%20have\%20some\%20feedback\%20regarding\%20the\%20section\%20\%27TODOs\%27\%20in\%20\%27pandoc\_writing\_tools\%20Feature\%20Demo\%27\%20v<VER>.\%0D\%0A\%0D\%0A}{\scriptsize\texttt{[Give Feedback]}}}}{TODOs }}\label{sec:todos}

It is often useful to be able to mark TODO items in the text. It helps
you to avoid writer's block on something you can't quite put into words
just yet. The \texttt{markup\_todo.lua} filter allows you to do this
with a \texttt{.todo} class. You can use this class on inline spans for
inline TODOs, or on divs for block TODOs. For example, please
\todospan{{fix this}} inline.

\tododiv{Block TODO content.}

\subsection{\texorpdfstring{Linking to github issues and edit links{
\href{mailto:docs@example.com?subject=\%5BFeedback\%5D\%20pandoc\_writing\_tools\%20Feature\%20Demo\%20v<VER>\%20-\%20Section\%3A\%20Linking\%20to\%20github\%20issues\%20and\%20edit\%20links\&body=Hi\%2C\%0D\%0A\%0D\%0AI\%20have\%20some\%20feedback\%20regarding\%20the\%20section\%20\%27Linking\%20to\%20github\%20issues\%20and\%20edit\%20links\%27\%20in\%20\%27pandoc\_writing\_tools\%20Feature\%20Demo\%27\%20v<VER>.\%0D\%0A\%0D\%0A}{\scriptsize\texttt{[Give Feedback]}}}}{Linking to github issues and edit links }}\label{sec:github-links}

Github issue link when enabled: Github issue {1}.

Edit links appear as little pencil icons at the end of headers when
\texttt{add\_edit\_to\_headers.lua} is enabled.

\printindex

\protect\phantomsection\label{refs}
\begin{CSLReferences}{1}{0}
\bibitem[\citeproctext]{ref-doe2020}
Doe, Jane. 2020. {``Demo Reference.''} \emph{Demo Journal}.

\end{CSLReferences}

\end{document}
